name: Peña Enmedio and Arroyo del Cabrón Trail
name_original: Ribera del arroyo Cabrón — running circular desde Figueruela de Arriba
description: This moderate 4.8-mile (7.7 km) circular jogging route from Figueruela de Arriba offers scenic views of the Sierra de la Culebra.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Figueruela De Arriba are set within a diverse landscape in the province of Zamora, Spain, characterized by its altitude of 858 meters and proximity to the Sierra de la Culebra. The region features varied terrain, including the Valle del Arroyo de la Fragua, and is shaped by the Manzanas and Cabrón rivers. Predominant holm oak forests and other native species contribute to a rich natural environment, offering numerous paths for running. Best jogging routes around Figueruela De Arriba The most popular jogging route is Peña Enmedio and Arroyo del Cabrón Trail, a 4.8 miles (7.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge with varied terrain near a stream and local hills. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from San Vitero, a moderate 5.7 miles (9.2 km) path. This route provides an accessible running experience through the rural landscapes surrounding the village of San Vitero. Local runners also love the Running loop from Boya, a 5.9 miles (9.6 km) trail leading through the natural surroundings of Boya, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Figueruela De Arriba is defined by its holm oak forests, river valleys, and the foothills of the Sierra de la Culebra. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate village loops to more challenging trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Figueruela De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of running routes can I find around Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The region offers a variety of running experiences, from moderate village loops to more challenging trails through diverse terrain. You'll find paths winding through holm oak forests, along river valleys, and into the foothills of the Sierra de la Culebra.

Q: How many running routes are available in the Figueruela De Arriba area?
A: There are 6 distinct running routes documented around Figueruela De Arriba. These include 4 moderate trails and 2 more difficult options, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate and difficult routes, the village itself offers accessible paths. For instance, the long street known as "La Salud" in Figueruela de Arriba provides a straightforward and accessible path for jogging within the village. The Senda Arroyo de las Truchas is also described as not overly strenuous.

Q: What are some of the natural highlights I might see while running in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Runners can enjoy stunning natural landscapes, including the rich wildlife of the Sierra de la Culebra, the varied topography of the Valle del Arroyo de la Fragua, and the lush environment shaped by the Manzanas and Cabrón rivers. You might also encounter holm oak forests and other native species.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the running trails in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Yes, the area boasts several scenic spots. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Mira Summit&lt;/a&gt;, the highest point in the Sierra de la Culebra, offers breathtaking panoramic vistas. You can also find views like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1956537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of São Julião in the Montesinho Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find running routes that are suitable for families or those with dogs?
A: Many of the natural paths and less strenuous village loops are suitable for families looking for an active outing. The tranquil environment of the Sierra de la Culebra and paths like the Senda Arroyo de las Truchas are generally welcoming for those running with dogs, provided they are kept under control and local regulations are followed.

Q: What is the longest running route available in this guide?
A: The longest running route featured is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio de Onor Village – Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor loop from Riomanzanas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 58 kilometers (36 miles). This is a difficult route, best suited for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Yes, several routes are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32133050" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from San Vitero&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Boya&lt;/a&gt; are popular circular options that allow you to start and finish in the same location.

Q: What kind of historical or cultural attractions can I explore near the running trails?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can discover charming villages like Villar de Ciervos and Manzanal de Arriba with their ancestral houses. In Figueruela de Arriba itself, the 13th-century Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7173693" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Stone Church&lt;/a&gt; adds a touch of historical interest.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: Over 40 runners have used komoot to explore Figueruela De Arriba's varied terrain. They often praise the region for its tranquil environment, diverse landscapes, and the opportunity to run amidst the natural beauty of the Sierra de la Culebra and its surrounding valleys.

Q: Are there any specific natural monuments or unique features to look out for?
A: Absolutely. Keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5814010" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Weir and Boardwalk of the Rio Onor&lt;/a&gt;, the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fidionda Spring&lt;/a&gt;, and the distinctive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6014548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carbón Stream and Antigua Losera de Figueruela&lt;/a&gt;, which offer unique natural experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Figueruela De Arriba?
A: The region's altitude of 858 meters provides invigorating air, making it pleasant for running during many seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings and evenings are also ideal to avoid the midday heat, while winter can offer crisp, clear conditions for those prepared for cooler weather.
tips: Not far from the town of Figueruela de Arriba, we find magnificent views of the landscape as we walk along this wide dirt road.
At this point, near the Cabrón stream, it is worth stopping for a few seconds to observe the views that this fabulous environment offers us.
Beautiful and picturesque wooden and stone bridge that allows us to cross the Cabrón stream.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this running route?
faqs.answer: Komoot classifies this route as a 'moderate run'. While it requires a good level of fitness, it is generally accessible for joggers looking for an engaging experience in the Sierra de la Culebra region.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Riverbank of the Cabrón stream running route?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily consists of paths, making up about 7.43 km of its length. You'll also find smaller sections of road (around 154 meters) and very short stretches of street. Expect unpaved sections, likely wide dirt roads, which are prevalent throughout the route.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Riverbank of the Cabrón stream trail?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail starting from Figueruela de Arriba. You should be able to find parking within the village itself, which serves as the convenient trailhead for this route.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this running route?
faqs.answer: This running route is approximately 7.66 km long with an elevation gain of about 211 meters. On average, it takes around 1 hour to complete, depending on your pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What natural features and views can I expect along the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect magnificent views of the Sierra de la Culebra, a mountain range known for its rich forest and diverse wildlife. The landscape features wooded slopes with native oak, chestnut, and pine trees, along with gullies and small streams. The Cabrón River itself is a small river nestled within these mountains, offering picturesque scenery.

faqs.question: Are there any specific highlights or landmarks to look out for?
faqs.answer: Yes, along the route, you'll encounter several notable points of interest. These include the View of the Sierra de la Culebra from the Trail, the View of the Arroyo del Cabrón, and the historic El Picón Bridges.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Culebra region is renowned for its rich fauna. While jogging, you might spot deer and wild boar. This area is also one of the last strongholds of the Iberian wolf, making it a significant spot for nature lovers, though direct sightings of wolves are rare.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run the Riverbank of the Cabrón stream trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beauty throughout the year, but spring and autumn are generally ideal. The weather is milder, and the natural colors of the Sierra de la Culebra are particularly vibrant, making for a more comfortable and scenic run.

faqs.question: Is the Riverbank of the Cabrón stream route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through a natural area known for wildlife, dogs are generally permitted. However, it is always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and enjoyment of all trail users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail or the Sierra de la Culebra area?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access this running route or the general Sierra de la Culebra area for recreational activities like jogging. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de debajo la Aldea, covering approximately 6% of its length.
